title: Herbertia caerulea
taxonomy: Herbertia caerulea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the iris family. It belongs to the genus Herbertia and is native to South America. Herbertia caerulea is further classified as a member of the subfamily Iridoideae and the tribe Irideae. This classification places it within the order Asparagales, which includes a diverse group of flowering plants. Herbertia caerulea's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the iris family.
uses: Herbertia caerulea is a plant with potential medicinal uses, but limited information is available. It is primarily used as a treatment for various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
